title: atropis palustris subsp. festuciformis
intro: Atropis palustris subsp. festuciformis, also known as the false hemlock or poison hemlock, is a plant species in the carrot family that has been used for its medicinal and health benefits. The plant is rich in thujone, a bioactive compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. Atropis palustris subsp. festuciformis has been traditionally used to relieve pain, reduce fever, and treat respiratory problems, as well as to support digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to exercise caution due to potential toxicity.
